flows.name: default
flows.description: Simple execution flow: execute, test, commit.

steps.name: execute
steps.content:
# EXECUTE

## PURPOSE

Implement the task described in your user prompt.

## WORKFLOW

1. Read the user prompt carefully
2. Understand what needs to be done
3. Implement the changes
4. Keep changes minimal and focused on the task

## RULES

- No git operations (commit, push, etc.)
- No scope expansion beyond what was asked

steps.name: test
steps.content:
# TEST

## PURPOSE

Verify your changes by running the project's test suite and fixing any failures.

## WORKFLOW

1. Run the project's test suite
2. If tests fail, read the error output carefully
3. Fix any failures caused by your changes
4. Re-run tests until all pass
5. If the project has no test suite, verify your changes by reading through them critically

## PERMITTED

- Run test commands
- Fix failing tests caused by your changes
- Modify test files if needed to reflect new behavior

## FORBIDDEN

- No new features or enhancements
- No git operations

steps.name: commit
steps.content:
# COMMIT

## PURPOSE

Stage and commit your changes.

## WORKFLOW

1. Stage all changed files: `git add -A`
2. Write a clear, concise commit message describing what was done and why
3. Commit: `git commit -m "<message>"`

## RULES

- Commit message must be descriptive (not just "fix" or "update")
- Do not push

gates.message: Working tree has uncommitted changes. Stage and commit all files before advancing.

flows.name: submit-pr
flows.description: Create a PR or comment on an existing one.

steps.name: submit-pr
steps.content:
# SUBMIT PR

## PURPOSE

Push your branch and either create a new PR or comment on an existing one.

## WORKFLOW

1. **Push the branch** to the remote:
   ```
   git push origin HEAD
   ```

2. **Check whether a PR already exists** for the current branch:
   ```
   gh pr view --json number,url 2>/dev/null
   ```

3. **If no PR exists** — create one:
   - Review changes: `git log main..HEAD --oneline` and `git diff main..HEAD --stat`
   - Write the PR body using the **PR Template** below
   - Create the PR:
     ```
     cat <<'EOF' > /tmp/pr-body.md
     <your filled-in PR body>
     EOF
     gh pr create --title "<descriptive title>" --body-file /tmp/pr-body.md
     ```

4. **If a PR already exists** — post a comment about the latest changes:
   - Review what changed since the last commit: `git log main..HEAD --oneline`
   - Write a concise comment describing what was done in this run
   - Post the comment:
     ```
     cat <<'EOF' > /tmp/pr-comment.md
     <your comment>
     EOF
     gh pr comment --body-file /tmp/pr-comment.md
     ```

## PR Template (for new PRs)

```markdown
## Summary

What was implemented and why.

## Changes

- `path/to/file.py` — what changed and why

## Test Results

Test commands run and results.
```

## RULES

- Always push before creating or commenting on a PR
- Always check for an existing PR first
- Fill every section with real content -- do not copy templates literally
- Be specific about what changed in this run

gates.command: test "$(git rev-parse HEAD)" = "$(git rev-parse origin/$(git branch --show-current) 2>/dev/null)"
gates.message: Local commits have not been pushed to the remote. Run git push origin HEAD before advancing.
